verses.text: Hearing the threat, Hezekiah tears his clothes, puts on sackcloth, goes to the house of the LORD, and sends to Isaiah, begging prayer for the remnant. The king turns at once to God. In crisis, faith's first move is to the temple and the prophet's intercession.

Hearing the threat, Hezekiah tears his clothes, puts on sackcloth, goes to the house of the LORD, and sends to Isaiah, begging prayer for the remnant. The king turns at once to God. In crisis, faith's first move is to the temple and the prophet's intercession.

Isaiah answers: fear not the words that blasphemed Me; I will send a blast, and Sennacherib will hear a rumour, return, and fall by the sword. God promises deliverance before a blow is struck. His bare word is enough to steady a trembling heart.

Isaiah answers: fear not the words that blasphemed Me; I will send a blast, and Sennacherib will hear a rumour, return, and fall by the sword. God promises deliverance before a blow is struck. His bare word is enough to steady a trembling heart.

Isaiah answers: fear not the words that blasphemed Me; I will send a blast, and Sennacherib will hear a rumour, return, and fall by the sword. God promises deliverance before a blow is struck. His bare word is enough to steady a trembling heart.

Sennacherib, hearing of an Egyptian threat, sends Hezekiah a letter renewing the taunt: let not your God deceive you. The pressure returns in writing. The enemy, beaten back from one assault, presses again to wear down faith with fear.

Sennacherib, hearing of an Egyptian threat, sends Hezekiah a letter renewing the taunt: let not your God deceive you. The pressure returns in writing. The enemy, beaten back from one assault, presses again to wear down faith with fear.

Sennacherib, hearing of an Egyptian threat, sends Hezekiah a letter renewing the taunt: let not your God deceive you. The pressure returns in writing. The enemy, beaten back from one assault, presses again to wear down faith with fear.

The letter boasts of all the lands and gods Assyria has destroyed — how then will Hezekiah escape? It is a catalogue of conquest meant to crush hope. Worldly power loves to recite its victories to make God's promises seem small.

The letter boasts of all the lands and gods Assyria has destroyed — how then will Hezekiah escape? It is a catalogue of conquest meant to crush hope. Worldly power loves to recite its victories to make God's promises seem small.

The letter boasts of all the lands and gods Assyria has destroyed — how then will Hezekiah escape? It is a catalogue of conquest meant to crush hope. Worldly power loves to recite its victories to make God's promises seem small.

Hezekiah takes the letter, goes up to the house of the LORD, and spreads it open before Him, praying, 'thou art the God, even thou alone.' He lays the threat literally before God. Faith does not hide its fears but spreads them out before the throne.

Hezekiah takes the letter, goes up to the house of the LORD, and spreads it open before Him, praying, 'thou art the God, even thou alone.' He lays the threat literally before God. Faith does not hide its fears but spreads them out before the throne.

Hezekiah takes the letter, goes up to the house of the LORD, and spreads it open before Him, praying, 'thou art the God, even thou alone.' He lays the threat literally before God. Faith does not hide its fears but spreads them out before the throne.

He admits Assyria has destroyed the nations and their gods — but those were wood and stone; now save us, that all may know Thou art the LORD alone. He prays for God's glory above his own rescue. The highest plea is that the world may know our God is God.

He admits Assyria has destroyed the nations and their gods — but those were wood and stone; now save us, that all may know Thou art the LORD alone. He prays for God's glory above his own rescue. The highest plea is that the world may know our God is God.

Isaiah sends word: God has heard, and answers Sennacherib's pride — the daughter of Zion despises you; whom have you reproached? the Holy One of Israel. The LORD takes the blasphemy personally. To threaten His people is to defy the Almighty Himself.

God declares He Himself ordained Assyria's conquests; Sennacherib was but His tool, and his raging is known — so He will put a hook in his nose and turn him back the way he came. The proud conqueror was only a rod in God's hand. He rules even those who defy Him.

God declares He Himself ordained Assyria's conquests; Sennacherib was but His tool, and his raging is known — so He will put a hook in his nose and turn him back the way he came. The proud conqueror was only a rod in God's hand. He rules even those who defy Him.

God gives a sign of recovery — the land will feed itself and bear fruit again — for a remnant shall go forth from Jerusalem; the zeal of the LORD will do this. Out of the crisis He promises survival and new growth. His zeal, not man's strength, secures the future.

God gives a sign of recovery — the land will feed itself and bear fruit again — for a remnant shall go forth from Jerusalem; the zeal of the LORD will do this. Out of the crisis He promises survival and new growth. His zeal, not man's strength, secures the future.

Sennacherib shall not enter the city, shoot an arrow, or cast up a siege bank, but return; for God will defend Jerusalem 'for mine own sake, and for my servant David's sake.' The deliverance rests on God's name and His covenant. He saves for His own glory and His promise.

Sennacherib shall not enter the city, shoot an arrow, or cast up a siege bank, but return; for God will defend Jerusalem 'for mine own sake, and for my servant David's sake.' The deliverance rests on God's name and His covenant. He saves for His own glory and His promise.

Sennacherib shall not enter the city, shoot an arrow, or cast up a siege bank, but return; for God will defend Jerusalem 'for mine own sake, and for my servant David's sake.' The deliverance rests on God's name and His covenant. He saves for His own glory and His promise.

That night the angel of the LORD strikes 185,000 in the Assyrian camp; Sennacherib goes home and is slain by his own sons as he worships his god. The mighty army falls without a battle, and the blasphemer dies before a powerless idol. God needs no army to humble the proud.
